LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PHILIP KIELY AND FILLMORE PLAZA APARTMENTS MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT SHOW DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH MALICE AND OPPRESSION, AND THAT THEIR CONDUCT WAS DESPICABLE. (SEE LACKNER VS. NORTH (2006) 135 CAL.APP. 4TH 1188.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEFENDANTS PHILIP KIELY AND FILLMORE PLAZA APARTMENTS' MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH COURT ORDER COMPELLING DISCOVERY; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Gregory M. Haynes, Esq. 546-0777 for pltf & opposing party; Jeanette N. Little, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates 267-3600 for defts & moving parties (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - Grant terminating sanction. The court dismisses the action with prejudice. Deny monetary sanction. No opposition filed. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order, to submit to plaintiff for approval as to form. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PHILIP KIELY, and, FILLMORE PLAZA APARTMENTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Jeanette N. Little, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates (415) 267-3600 appearing for Defendants and moving party; Gregory Haynes, Esq. of Law Offices of Gregory M. Haynes (415) 546-0777 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. No allegations of extreme and outrageous conduct are pleaded to support a claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open Court.(302/EHG)Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pltf'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PHILIP KIELY, and, FILLMORE PLAZA APARTMENTS Motion To Strike Portions of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Appearances: Gregory Haynes, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 546-0777 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jeanette N. Little, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Assoc. (415) 267-3600 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted, with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege facts that show defendants acted with malice and oppression, and that their conduct was despicable. (See Lackner vs. North (2006) 135 Cal.App. 4th 1188.) Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
